source_anchors.what_text_says: The Skill is triggered by requests to spec, file an issue, write a ticket, or create a backlog item.
does_not_support: It does not support using spec as a universal workflow for all engineering tasks.

source_anchors.what_text_says: The Skill files the issue and may connect to a broader source-issue lifecycle.
does_not_support: It does not prove the lifecycle was executed in this demo.

source_anchors.what_text_says: The source performs extensive update, session, branch, config, telemetry, and environment checks.
does_not_support: These checks are not the main reader body.

source_anchors.what_text_says: Skill instructions take precedence in plan mode and may reach STOP points or decision prompts.
does_not_support: It does not mean ReaderLab should run the Skill.

source_anchors.what_text_says: Every decision prompt must include issue explanation, stakes, recommendation, completeness, pros and cons, and a net tradeoff.
does_not_support: It does not require every trivial choice to use the full pattern.

source_anchors.source_ref: When AskUserQuestion is unavailable or a call fails
source_anchors.what_text_says: The workflow distinguishes auto-decide, genuine failure, spawned, headless, and interactive fallback.
does_not_support: It does not license silent user-consent fabrication.

source_anchors.what_text_says: The workflow forbids dropping real options to fit tool limits and requires batching or splitting.
does_not_support: It does not prove all candidate options are equally important.

source_anchors.what_text_says: The source includes long-running tool maintenance, privacy, and artifact sync behavior.
does_not_support: It does not justify adopting those behaviors in ReaderLab.

source_anchors.what_text_says: The workflow requires answering who is affected, current behavior, desired behavior, why now, and completion signal before proceeding.
does_not_support: It does not permit filing an issue after the first user message.

source_anchors.what_text_says: The workflow locks out-of-scope items, touched systems, ordering constraints, MVP cut, failure modes, and rollback options.
does_not_support: It does not allow uncontrolled scope expansion.

source_anchors.what_text_says: Before asking technical questions, the workflow must read codebase evidence and cite what it found.
does_not_support: It does not require broad scans when narrow evidence is enough.

source_anchors.what_text_says: The workflow files an issue, archives the spec locally, and may spawn an agent in a fresh worktree depending on flags and plan mode.
does_not_support: It does not say this ReaderLab demo should file or spawn anything.

baseline_summary_trap: A shallow summary would say: this Skill creates specs and GitHub issues and includes many setup commands. That misses the real design problem: preserving user decision authority while converting vague intent into executable work.
upgrade_question: 在 agent 能把需求快速推进的约束下，如何把模糊用户意图转成可执行规格，同时避免自动化吞掉范围、风险和用户决策？

input: Vague request to spec an idea, file an issue, write a ticket, or create a backlog item
constraint: Session may be interactive, headless, or spawned
Runtime shell can pollute the method
action: Read project evidence before technical questions
File, archive, and optionally hand off execution
Separate core workflow from runtime shell
output: ReaderLab cleaned body and design assets
boundary: No command execution in this demo

lens_candidates.clarifies: Shows spec as a gate that converts vague intent into accountable execution input.
lens_candidates.boundary_power: Keeps spec from becoming every workflow.
lens_candidates.reader_gain: Reader sees why the core is decision protection, not issue writing.
lens_candidates.overreach_risk: Could overstate authorial intent if not phrased as interpretation.

lens_candidates.text_trigger: Preamble, telemetry, routing, vendoring, artifact sync
lens_candidates.clarifies: Explains why much of the source should be treated as operational shell rather than body.
lens_candidates.boundary_power: Prevents runtime details from becoming ReaderLab defaults.
lens_candidates.reader_gain: Reader understands why cleaned body is not a summary.
lens_candidates.overreach_risk: May remove too much if audit does not preserve safety categories.

lens_candidates.text_trigger: Fallback, headless, spawned, and destructive confirmation rules
lens_candidates.clarifies: Shows how the workflow avoids pretending that continuation equals consent.
lens_candidates.reader_gain: Reader sees the safety reason behind verbose decision prompts.
lens_candidates.overreach_risk: Could make all automation look unsafe if stated too broadly.

lens_candidates.text_trigger: gstack local paths, Claude-specific routing, artifact sync
lens_candidates.clarifies: Can warn against adopting a foreign runtime wholesale.
lens_candidates.reader_gain: Useful warning but not the central explanation.
lens_candidates.overreach_risk: Could become a generic anti-tool claim not supported by source.

absorb: Use decision-brief structure for high-impact scope, data, output, or state-changing choices.
Separate engineering material into cleaned body plus audit shell.
downgrade: Use environment-specific behavior only when the workflow genuinely crosses interactive, headless, and child-agent contexts.
Treat decision-brief generation as a candidate Skill only after independent trigger/input/output evidence is proven.
reject: Do not adopt gstack runtime setup, telemetry, routing injection, or artifact sync as ReaderLab defaults.
Do not execute the source Skill commands during this reading demo.

reader_facing_explanation.title: 读这份 spec，重点不是命令，而是它怎样保护需求不变形

delta_eval.reader_gain: Reader should understand the Skill as a decision-protection mechanism for vague requirements, not as a command-heavy issue generator.
